sections.title: Market Pulse: Cautious Optimism Ahead of Fed and Tech Earnings

items.headline: U.S. Stock Futures Edge Higher as Investors Await Fed Decision and Big Tech Earnings
items.summary: U.S. stock futures showed modest gains this Wednesday morning as market participants prepared for a crucial day, featuring a Federal Reserve interest rate decision and earnings reports from major technology companies. However, this follows a downturn on Tuesday, driven by concerns over AI sector performance and ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Overall market sentiment remains cautiously optimistic amidst mixed economic signals.

sections.title: Top Movers (As of April 29, 2026, where available)

items.headline: Seagate Technology Soars on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Shares of Seagate Technology surged after the storage company reported Q3 results and guidance that significantly surpassed Wall Street's estimates.

items.headline: NXP Semiconductors Climbs on Robust Q1 Performance
items.summary: NXP Semiconductors N.V. stock climbed after the analog semiconductor company's Q1 results and guidance topped analyst estimates by a significant margin.

items.headline: Bloom Energy Jumps After Q1 Beat and Raised Outlook
items.summary: Bloom Energy shares surged following a strong Q1 beat and an upward revision of its FY2026 outlook.

items.headline: Visa Shares Jump on Q2 Profit Beat and Raised Forecast
items.summary: Visa shares climbed in premarket trading after beating second-quarter profit estimates and lifting full-year earnings expectations, buoyed by resilient consumer spending.

items.headline: Robinhood Markets Falls Short on Q1 Revenue and EPS
items.summary: Robinhood Markets Inc. stock dropped after its first-quarter revenue and earnings per share missed Street consensus estimates.

items.headline: Arm Holdings Tumbles Amid AI Semiconductor Concerns
items.summary: Arm Holdings plc experienced a significant decline on Tuesday, contributing to broader weakness among AI semiconductor bigwigs.

items.headline: Avis Budget Group Shares Decrease Following Q1 Results
items.summary: Avis Budget Group Inc.'s stock price decreased in pre-market trading after the company released its first-quarter 2026 results.

items.headline: Fed Expected to Hold Rates Steady in Powell's Final Meeting
items.summary: The Federal Reserve is widely expected to maintain its current interest rate this Wednesday, marking what is likely Jerome Powell's last policy meeting as chairman. The FOMC's policy statement is anticipated at 2:00 p.m. ET, followed by a press conference. Market focus is also on the Senate committee vote for Kevin Warsh, President Trump's nominee to succeed Powell.

items.headline: Four Major Tech Giants to Report Q1 Earnings After Market Close
items.summary: Alphabet ($GOOGL), Amazon ($AMZN), Meta Platforms ($META), and Microsoft ($MSFT) are all scheduled to release their first-quarter earnings reports after the market closes today. Investors will be scrutinizing these reports for evidence that substantial AI investments are translating into revenue growth.

items.headline: UAE Announces Exit from OPEC, Driving Oil Prices Higher
items.summary: The United Arab Emirates has announced its withdrawal from the OPEC oil cartel, effective May 1, a move that has introduced new volatility into global energy markets. Brent crude and WTI crude prices notably rose by over 3% in early trading today.

items.headline: Consumer Confidence Rises, Trade Deficit Widens in March
items.summary: The Conference Board reported an increase in the consumer confidence index for April to 92.8, surpassing expectations. Meanwhile, the U.S. Census Bureau's Monthly Advance Economic Indicators Report for March 2026 showed the international trade deficit widened to $87.9 billion, and wholesale and retail inventories saw increases of 1.4% and 0.7% respectively.

items.headline: Mixed Sector Performance; AI Concerns Weigh on Tech
items.summary: On Tuesday, the S&P 500 saw mixed sector performance, with six out of eleven sectors closing down. Consumer Staples ($XLP) fell 1.1%, while Financials ($XLF) gained 0.8%. AI-related stocks were notable decliners, reflecting ongoing concerns about the AI trade. Technology, energy, and financials are projected to be the primary drivers of S&P 500 earnings this year.
